text:
There is something in a human that triggers when it sees a thought being worked out in real time.

Not explained. Worked out. The difference matters.

When Caleb was solving a problem in front of me, I was not just watching his hands. I was watching his mind. Where he paused. What he reconsidered. The moment he saw something I had not seen yet.

That is a different kind of learning. Not information transfer. Pattern recognition at a level you cannot get from a page.

You absorb it without knowing you are absorbing it. And then one day your hands know something your mouth cannot explain.

That is what real time feedback does. It builds something in you that memorization cannot reach.
